risk: Lactic acidosis risk during imaging procedures
action: Stop Metformin 48 hours before contrast administration. Resume 48 hours after if renal function stable.

risk: Myopathy — Amlodipine inhibits Simvastatin metabolism, increasing plasma levels and muscle damage risk
action: Limit Simvastatin dose to 20mg/day when combined with Amlodipine. Monitor for muscle pain or weakness.

risk: Severe bleeding risk — dual anticoagulant effect
action: Monitor INR closely. Use lowest effective Aspirin dose. Consider GI prophylaxis.

risk: Ferrous Sulphate reduces Levothyroxine absorption by up to 50% if taken together
action: Take medications at least 4 hours apart. Monitor TSH levels closely.

risk: Calcium forms insoluble complex with Levothyroxine, reducing absorption
action: Take at least 4 hours apart. Monitor TSH.

risk: Hyperkalaemia — dangerously high potassium, cardiac arrhythmia risk
action: Monitor serum potassium weekly. Avoid potassium supplements unless deficient.

risk: NSAIDs reduce Methotrexate renal clearance — toxicity risk
action: Avoid combination. If necessary, monitor methotrexate levels and renal function closely.

risk: Serotonin syndrome — life-threatening hyperthermia, agitation, seizures
action: Absolute contraindication. Washout period of 14 days required between classes.

risk: Antacids reduce Ciprofloxacin absorption by chelation
action: Take Ciprofloxacin 2 hours before or 6 hours after antacids.

risk: Clarithromycin inhibits statin metabolism — rhabdomyolysis risk
action: Suspend statin during Clarithromycin course. Resume 5 days after completion.

risk: Amiodarone increases Digoxin levels — toxicity: nausea, arrhythmia, vision changes
action: Reduce Digoxin dose by 50% when starting Amiodarone. Monitor levels closely.

risk: Beta-blockers mask hypoglycaemia symptoms — tachycardia blunted. Sweating persists as warning.
action: Monitor blood glucose closely. Educate patient on non-cardiac hypoglycaemia symptoms.

risk: Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19 — reduces Clopidogrel antiplatelet effect by up to 40%
action: Switch to Pantoprazole for GI prophylaxis. Avoid Omeprazole in patients on Clopidogrel.

risk: Severe hypotension — potentially fatal. Both lower BP via nitric oxide pathway
action: Absolute contraindication. Do not administer nitrates within 24-48 hours of Sildenafil.

risk: Rifampicin induces CYP3A4 — reduces OCP hormone levels, contraceptive failure risk
action: Use barrier contraception during Rifampicin course and for 4 weeks after.

risk: NSAIDs reduce renal Lithium clearance — toxicity: tremor, confusion, renal damage
action: Avoid NSAIDs in Lithium-treated patients. Use Paracetamol for pain.

risk: Paroxetine inhibits CYP2D6 — reduces conversion of Tamoxifen to active metabolite Endoxifen
action: Use alternative antidepressant (Venlafaxine). Avoid strong CYP2D6 inhibitors.

risk: Phenytoin depletes folate stores — risk of megaloblastic anaemia and neural tube defects
action: Supplement Folic Acid 5mg daily in all patients on Phenytoin, especially women of childbearing age.

risk: Additive QT prolongation — risk of Torsades de Pointes, fatal ventricular arrhythmia
action: Obtain baseline ECG. Avoid combination where possible. Monitor QTc if necessary.

risk: Disulfiram-like reaction — severe nausea, vomiting, flushing, tachycardia
action: Strict alcohol avoidance during treatment and 48 hours after completing Metronidazole course.
